question.text: When was the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) established, marking the beginning of forward contracts in organized trading?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) was established in 1848 and began facilitating forward contracts in agricultural commodities. This is considered the beginning of organized derivative trading. **The Trap:** 1865 is when CBOT introduced the first exchange-traded futures contracts (standardized). 1919 is when CME was established. 1973 is when CBOE started listed options trading. **Cross-Exam Context:** Historical milestones are tested in NISM Series VIII. The timeline: 1848 (CBOT forwards) -> 1865 (CBOT futures) -> 1919 (CME) -> 1972 (IMM currency futures) -> 1973 (CBOE options). **Key Takeaway:** CBOT 1848 = forwards; CBOT 1865 = first exchange-traded futures. Remember: forwards came first, futures came later as standardized versions.
